commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Feishu reply dispatch so oversized final text bypasses streaming cards, discards active previews, and adds dispatcher tests for chunked final delivery.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +52. Total +55 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main forces final Feishu streaming replies into CardKit before chunking, and the PR branch still leaves over-limit auto markdown/table finals eligible for that same streaming path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow mechanical repair remains: the over-limit guard must suppress streaming for auto markdown/table finals before this PR can fix the Feishu message-loss bug.

target.title: fix(feishu): preserve long streaming replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep oversized Feishu final replies off the streaming-card path when they exceed the configured text chunk limit.
- Discard any active streaming preview before delivering the oversized final reply through the existing chunked message/card send path.
- Add focused coverage for oversized plain final replies and partial-preview cleanup.

Fixes #88631.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main`
- One-shot local Feishu-compatible HTTP proof harness using the real dispatcher, real chunker, real `sendMessageFeishu`, and the real Lark SDK `Client.im.message.create` request path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu streaming no longer routes oversized plain final replies into a single streaming card element that can exceed CardKit content limits.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout with a one-shot Feishu-compatible HTTP endpoint. The proof exercised `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/send.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/client.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.ts`, and the installed `@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk` client request path. This is real OpenClaw/Lark SDK code-path proof, not a mocked unit test and not a live Feishu tenant.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the one-shot Node proof harness, then reran the focused Vitest file and formatting/diff checks listed above.

Evidence after fix: For an oversized final with no partial preview, the harness recorded `post_chunk_count=3`, `post_chunk_lengths=10,10,3`, `interactive_create_calls=0`, and `cardkit_calls=0`; the HTTP paths were one token request plus three `POST /open-apis/im/v1/messages?receive_id_type=chat_id` post sends. For an oversized final after an active partial preview, the harness recorded streaming start, `DELETE /open-apis/im/v1/messages/om_3`, then the same three chunked post sends.

Observed result after fix: Oversized final text is delivered as chunked Feishu post messages (`"0123456789"`, `"abcdefghij"`, `"XYZ"`) instead of closing into a streaming card. Existing partial previews are explicitly discarded before final chunked delivery.

What was not tested: A live Feishu tenant/CardKit API call was not run; this checkout has no configured Feishu/Lark app credentials or target, and repo search found no Feishu secret-backed live lane.
target.author: monkeyleet

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
